dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {target_npc} refused to see me. Slammed the door and told me to leave before they did something they'd regret.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I expected anger, but the depth of it shook me. {target_npc} said the {quest_focus} destroyed more than just their standing: it cost them friends, livelihood, years they'll never get back. They threw the {quest_item} I brought into the dirt. The {enemy_type} have moved closer to where {target_npc} is sheltering {location_hint}, and if they stay out of stubbornness, they'll be in real danger.
dialogueChunks.outro: Words failed. Gifts failed. The only thing left is action that speaks louder than either. The {quest_stakes} still {quest_stakes_has} value.

dialogueChunks.intro: The confession didn't go the way I hoped, {player_name}. The truth came out, and now everyone knows.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When I admitted what I did to the council, the reaction was exactly what I deserved. They stripped my standing and half the settlement won't look at me. But {target_npc} heard about the confession and sent word: not forgiveness, but a request. They need {quest_item} to defend against the {enemy_type} that have been threatening their position. The {quest_threat} {quest_threat_is} closing in.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement turned its back on me. But {target_npc} opened a door, just a crack. I won't waste it.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the past just caught up with us in the worst possible way.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is in danger. The {enemy_type} overran the area {location_hint} where they've been living, and they sent a desperate message asking for help. The irony isn't lost on me: the person I wronged is now begging for rescue. I want to go myself, but my presence would do more harm than good right now.
dialogueChunks.outro: I need you to bring {quest_item} and clear the {enemy_type} so {target_npc} can escape safely. If they survive because of me, maybe that starts to balance the scales. The fate of the {quest_stakes} hangs on it.
